items.front: Formula for axial deformation of a prismatic member?
items.hint: Derived from Hooke's law: σ=Eε and ε=δ/L

items.front: Polar moment of inertia of a solid circular shaft of diameter d?
items.hint: Compare to the rectangular I = bh³/12 — different shape, different formula

items.front: Maximum torsional shear stress in a solid circular shaft, in terms of T and d only?
items.hint: Substitute r=d/2 and J=πd⁴/32 into τ=Tr/J and simplify

items.front: Units of the angle of twist φ = TL/(JG) when T, L, J, G are in consistent SI units?
items.back: Radians (must multiply by 180/π to convert to degrees)
items.hint: This is the single most common units mistake in torsion problems

items.front: Flexure formula for bending stress, and what does c represent?
items.back: σ = Mc/I; c is the distance from the neutral axis to the extreme (outer) fiber
items.hint: For a symmetric rectangular section, c = h/2
